**Biological Interpretation:** PM-specific processes like midline fusion.
Found `r length(genes_PM_only_up)` increasing and `r length(genes_PM_only_down)` decreasing.

```{r}
#| label: pm-only-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

b6_domain_slopes_clean |>
  filter(gene_id %in% c(genes_PM_only_up, genes_PM_only_down), Domain == "PM") |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, Domain, log2FoldChange, padj) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange))) |>
  create_dt()
```

Over-represented pathways and biological processes:

```{r}
#| label: pm-only-ora
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: hide
#| cache: true

# Run enrichment on both sets
genes_PM_only_up_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_PM_only_up,
                                     description = "PM only increasing"
                                     )

genes_PM_only_down_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_PM_only_down,
                                     description = "PM only decreasing"
                                     )

```

```{r}
#| label: pm-only-ora-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
genes_PM_only_up_ora$result |> 
  rbind(
    genes_PM_only_down_ora$result
  ) |> 
  create_dt()
```

### MAX Only

```{r}
#| label: max-only-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false

genes_MAX_only_up <- gene_assignments |>
  filter(MAX_only_up) |>
  pull(gene_id)

genes_MAX_only_down <- domain_dynamics |>
  filter(!PM_dynamic, MAX_dynamic, !POST_dynamic, MAX_slope < -0.5) |>
  pull(gene_id)

if(length(genes_MAX_only_up) > 0) {
  n_show <- ifelse(length(genes_MAX_only_up) < 15, length(genes_MAX_only_up), 3)
  plot_domain_specific_dynamics(
    gene_ids = genes_MAX_only_up,
    domain_name = "MAX",
    direction = "Increasing",
    count_data = b6_gene_counts,
    slope_data = b6_domain_slopes_clean,
    n = n_show
  )
}
```

**Pattern:** Developmental changes only in MAX domain.

**Biological Interpretation:** MAX-specific processes like lateral shelf morphogenesis.
Found `r length(genes_MAX_only_up)` increasing and `r length(genes_MAX_only_down)` decreasing.

```{r}
#| label: max-only-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

b6_domain_slopes_clean |>
  filter(gene_id %in% c(genes_MAX_only_up, genes_MAX_only_down), Domain == "MAX") |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, Domain, log2FoldChange, padj) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange))) |>
  create_dt()
```

Over-represented pathways and biological processes:

```{r}
#| label: max-only-ora
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: hide
#| cache: true

# Run enrichment on both sets
genes_MAX_only_up_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_MAX_only_up,
                                     description = "MAX only increasing"
                                     )

genes_MAX_only_down_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_MAX_only_down,
                                     description = "MAX only decreasing"
                                     )
```


```{r}
#| label: max-only-ora-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
genes_MAX_only_up_ora$result |> 
  rbind(
    genes_MAX_only_down_ora$result
  ) |> 
  create_dt()
```

### POST Only

```{r}
#| label: post-only-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false

genes_POST_only_up <- gene_assignments |>
  filter(POST_only_up) |>
  pull(gene_id)

genes_POST_only_down <- domain_dynamics |>
  filter(!PM_dynamic, !MAX_dynamic, POST_dynamic, POST_slope < -0.5) |>
  pull(gene_id)

if(length(genes_POST_only_up) > 0) {
  n_show <- ifelse(length(genes_POST_only_up) < 15, length(genes_POST_only_up), 3)
  plot_domain_specific_dynamics(
    gene_ids = genes_POST_only_up,
    domain_name = "POST",
    direction = "Increasing",
    count_data = b6_gene_counts,
    slope_data = b6_domain_slopes_clean,
    n = n_show
  )
}
```

**Pattern:** Developmental changes only in POST domain.

**Biological Interpretation:** Posterior-specific programs like soft palate differentiation.
Found `r length(genes_POST_only_up)` increasing and `r length(genes_POST_only_down)` decreasing.

```{r}
#| label: post-only-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

b6_domain_slopes_clean |>
  filter(gene_id %in% c(genes_POST_only_up, genes_POST_only_down), Domain == "POST") |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, Domain, log2FoldChange, padj) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange))) |>
  create_dt()
```

Over-represented pathways and biological processes:

```{r}
#| label: post-only-ora
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: hide
#| cache: true

# Run enrichment on both sets
genes_POST_only_up_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_POST_only_up,
                                     description = "POST only increasing"
                                     )

genes_POST_only_down_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_POST_only_down,
                                     description = "POST only decreasing"
                                     )

```

```{r}
#| label: post-only-ora-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
genes_POST_only_up_ora$result |> 
  rbind(
    genes_POST_only_down_ora$result
  ) |> 
  create_dt()
```
:::

## Complex Patterns

**Genes showing particularly complex spatiotemporal dynamics** including opposite trajectories or three-way divergence.

::: {#B6_DESeq2_comp_pat .panel-tabset .nav-pills}
### Overview

```{r}
#| label: complex-overview
#| warning: false
#| message: false

pattern_summary |>
  filter(Category == "Complex") |>
  arrange(desc(N_Genes)) |>
  create_dt()
```

### Opposite Slopes

```{r}
#| label: opposite-slopes-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false

genes_opposite <- gene_assignments |>
  filter(Opposite_slopes) |>
  pull(gene_id)

if(length(genes_opposite) > 0) {
  n_show <- ifelse(length(genes_opposite) < 15, length(genes_opposite), 5)
  plot_simple_trajectories(
    gene_ids = genes_opposite,
    count_data = b6_gene_counts,
    title_text = paste0("Genes with Opposite Slopes (n=", length(genes_opposite), ")"),
    n = n_show
  )
}
```

**Pattern:** `r length(genes_opposite)` genes where at least one domain increases while another decreases.

**Biological Interpretation:** Fundamentally different developmental programs between domains, potentially key regulators of domain identity.

```{r}
#| label: opposite-slopes-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

b6_domain_slopes_clean |>
  filter(gene_id %in% genes_opposite) |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, Domain, log2FoldChange, padj) |>
  arrange(gene_id, Domain) |>
  create_dt()
```

Over-represented pathways and biological processes:

```{r}
#| label: opposite-slopes-ora
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: hide
#| cache: true

genes_opposite_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_opposite,
                                     description = "Opposite slopes"
                                     )
```

```{r}
#| label: opposite-slopes-ora-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
genes_opposite_ora$result |> 
  create_dt()
```

### Three-Way Divergent

```{r}
#| label: three-way-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false

genes_three_way <- gene_assignments |>
  filter(Three_way) |>
  pull(gene_id)

if(length(genes_three_way) > 0) {
  n_show <- ifelse(length(genes_three_way) < 15, length(genes_three_way), 5)
  plot_simple_trajectories(
    gene_ids = genes_three_way,
    count_data = b6_gene_counts,
    title_text = paste0("Three-Way Divergent Genes (n=", length(genes_three_way), ")"),
    n = n_show
  )
}
```

**Pattern:** `r length(genes_three_way)` genes where all three domains show different developmental slopes.

**Biological Interpretation:** Maximum spatiotemporal complexity, likely central regulators of domain-specific identity and function.

```{r}
#| label: three-way-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

b6_domain_slopes_clean |>
  filter(gene_id %in% genes_three_way) |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, Domain, log2FoldChange, padj) |>
  arrange(gene_id, Domain) |>
  create_dt()
```

Over-represented pathways and biological processes:

```{r}
#| label: three-way-ora
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: hide
#| cache: true

genes_three_way_ora <- run_enrichment_hybrid(gene_ids = genes_three_way,
                                     description = "Three-way divergent"
                                     )
```

```{r}
#| label: three-way-ora-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
genes_three_way_ora$result |> 
  create_dt()
```
:::

## Pattern Overlap Analysis

::: {#B6_DESeq2_pattern_overlap .panel-tabset .nav-pills}
### Multi-Pattern Genes

```{r}
#| label: pattern-overlap
#| warning: false
#| message: false

multi_pattern_genes <- gene_assignments |>
  filter(n_patterns > 1) |>
  arrange(desc(n_patterns))

multi_pattern_genes |>
  select(symbol, n_patterns) |>
  group_by(n_patterns) |>
  summarize(n_genes = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  create_dt()
```

### Top Multi-Pattern Genes

```{r}
#| label: top-multi-pattern
#| warning: false
#| message: false

multi_pattern_genes |>
  left_join(all_genes_palate |> select(gene_id, chromosome), by = "gene_id") |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, n_patterns) |>
  head(50) |>
  create_dt()
```

### Pattern Overlap UpSet Plot

```{r}
#| label: pattern-upset-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.width: 14
#| fig.height: 10

# Prepare data for upset plot - select key pattern categories
upset_data <- gene_assignments |>
  select(
    gene_id,
    # Domain main effects
    MAX_higher_PM, MAX_lower_PM,
    POST_higher_PM, POST_lower_PM,
    POST_higher_MAX, POST_lower_MAX,
    Highest_MAX, Highest_POST, Highest_PM,
    AP_decreasing, AP_increasing,
    # Stage main effects
    Dev_increasing, Dev_decreasing,
    # Diverging
    MAX_div_PM, POST_div_PM, POST_div_MAX,
    # Converging
    MAX_conv_PM, POST_conv_PM, POST_conv_MAX,
    # Domain-specific
    PM_only_up, MAX_only_up, POST_only_up,
    # Complex
    Opposite_slopes, Three_way
  ) |>
  # Convert to data frame and set gene_id as rownames
  as.data.frame() |>
  column_to_rownames("gene_id") |>
  # Convert logical to numeric
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Create upset plot
UpSetR::upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  order.by = "freq",
  decreasing = TRUE,
  mb.ratio = c(0.6, 0.4),
  number.angles = 0,
  point.size = 3,
  line.size = 1,
  mainbar.y.label = "Pattern Intersections",
  sets.x.label = "Genes per Pattern",
  text.scale = c(1.5, 1.2, 1.2, 1.2, 1.5, 1.2),
  set_size.show = TRUE,
  keep.order = TRUE,
  sets.bar.color = "#56B4E9"
)
```

This UpSet plot visualizes: - The size of each pattern category (left bar chart) - Which pattern combinations occur most frequently (top bar chart) - The degree of overlap between different patterns (connection lines) - Genes with multiple pattern assignments
:::
::::::::::

# Comprehensive Tables

::: {#B6_DESeq2_tables .panel-tabset .nav-pills}
## All Patterns Summary

```{r}
#| label: all-patterns-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

pattern_summary |>
  create_dt()
```

## Gene-Level Pattern Assignments

```{r}
#| label: gene-assignments-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false

gene_assignments |>
  left_join(all_genes_palate |> select(gene_id, chromosome), by = "gene_id") |>
  select(gene_id, symbol, chromosome, n_patterns, everything()) |>
  create_dt()
```

## Over-representation analysis summary

```{r}
#| label: combined-ora-results
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Collect all ORA results and combine them
all_ora_results <- bind_rows(
  # Domain Main Effects
  genes_MAX_higher_than_PM_ora$result,
  genes_MAX_lower_than_PM_ora$result,
  genes_POST_higher_than_PM_ora$result,
  genes_POST_lower_than_PM_ora$result,
  genes_POST_higher_than_MAX_ora$result,
  genes_POST_lower_than_MAX_ora$result,
  genes_MAX_highest_ora$result,
  genes_POST_highest_ora$result,
  genes_PM_highest_ora$result,
  genes_AP_decreasing_ora$result,
  genes_AP_increasing_ora$result,
  
  # Stage Main Effects
  dev_increasing_enrichment_results$result,
  dev_decreasing_enrichment_results$result,
  
  # Diverging Trajectories
  genes_MAX_div_ora$result,
  genes_POST_div_ora$result,
  genes_POST_div_MAX_ora$result,
  
  # Converging Trajectories
  genes_MAX_conv_ora$result,
  genes_POST_conv_ora$result,
  genes_POST_conv_MAX_ora$result,
  
  # Domain-Specific Dynamics
  genes_PM_only_up_ora$result,
  genes_PM_only_down_ora$result,
  genes_MAX_only_up_ora$result,
  genes_MAX_only_down_ora$result,
  genes_POST_only_up_ora$result,
  genes_POST_only_down_ora$result,
  
  # Complex Patterns
  genes_opposite_ora$result,
  genes_three_way_ora$result
) |>
  # Add category grouping for easier filtering
  mutate(
    Category = case_when(
      gene_set %in% c("MAX higher than PM", "MAX lower than PM", 
                      "POST higher than PM", "POST lower than PM",
                      "POST higher than MAX", "POST lower than MAX",
                      "MAX highest", "POST highest", "PM highest",
                      "A→P decreasing", "A→P increasing") ~ "Domain Main Effects",
      gene_set %in% c("Increase", "Decrease") ~ "Stage Main Effects",
      gene_set %in% c("MAX diverging from PM", "POST diverging from PM", 
                      "POST diverging from MAX") ~ "Diverging Trajectories",
      gene_set %in% c("MAX converging to PM", "POST converging to PM", 
                      "POST converging to MAX") ~ "Converging Trajectories",
      gene_set %in% c("PM only increasing", "PM only decreasing",
                      "MAX only increasing", "MAX only decreasing",
                      "POST only increasing", "POST only decreasing") ~ "Domain-Specific Dynamics",
      gene_set %in% c("Opposite slopes", "Three-way divergent") ~ "Complex Patterns",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) |>
  # Reorder columns
  select(Category, gene_set, source, term_id, term_name, p_value, 
         query_size, term_size, intersection_size, intersection) |>
  filter( p_value < 0.01) |> 
  arrange(Category, gene_set, p_value)
```

```{r}
#| label: combined-ora-results-table
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Display the combined results
all_ora_results |>
  create_dt()

# # Summary by category
# all_ora_results |>
#   group_by(Category, gene_set) |>
#   summarize(
#     n_enriched_terms = n(),
#     .groups = "drop"
#   ) |>
#   arrange(Category, desc(n_enriched_terms)) |>
#   create_dt()

```

:::

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
